---
title: "CV"
---

::: {.panel .panel-light}
## Education

**East China Normal University.** M.Sc. in Human Geography, 2023-2026. School of Geographic Sciences; research training in economic geography, regional innovation, scientific mobility, and urban networks.

**Northeast Normal University.** B.Sc. in Human Geography and Urban-Rural Planning, 2019-2023. Training in quantitative geography, GIS applications, urban geography, regional analysis, and planning.
:::

::: {.panel .panel-muted}
## Research Outputs

- **Spatial Structure and Determinants of Top Global Biomedical Scientists' Mobility from an Urban Network Perspective** — accepted article.
- **Pre-move Publication-Location Exposure and Destination Choice among Elite Biomedical Scientists** — working paper in revision.
- **Intercity Mobility Network of Top Global Biomedical Scientists: A Dual Perspective of Whole and Ego Networks** — master’s thesis.
- **Author-Institution Linkage and Geocoding Feasibility Audit** — public research repository.
:::

::: {.panel .panel-light}
## Selected Research Experience

::: {.project-list}
::: {.project-card}
### Scientific Mobility, Knowledge Networks, and Urban Innovation

Flagship research programme linking elite scientist mobility, knowledge-space connections, collaboration networks, and urban innovation conditions.
:::

::: {.project-card}
### Daily Nighttime Lights and Urban Resilience Trajectories

Collaborative research using VIIRS-DNB daily nighttime lights, Fisher Information, dynamic time warping, and clustering to describe urban resilience trajectories.
:::

::: {.project-card}
### Talent Mobility and Biomedical Innovation Systems in China and the United States

Ongoing comparative research on top scientist training, career mobility, research output, patents, industrial foundations, and knowledge translation.
:::

::: {.project-card}
### Global Trade Network Evolution and Sectoral Differences under the COVID-19 Shock

Earlier research using directed weighted trade networks and spatial analysis to compare structural changes in global commodity trade.
:::

::: {.project-card}
### Author-Institution Linkage and Geocoding Feasibility Audit

Public workflow audit for linkage, institution normalization, geocoding feasibility, and coverage diagnostics using synthetic records.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.panel .panel-dark}
## Methods and Technical Skills

Python; R; Stata; GIS; ArcGIS; NetworkX; Gephi; Ucinet; Conditional Logit; ERGM/WERGM; Social Network Analysis; Ego-network Analysis; Entity Resolution; Longitudinal Multi-source Data Construction.
:::

::: {.panel .panel-light}
## Download Full CV

::: {.cv-download}
The full public CV is available as a PDF.

[Download Full CV](assets/cv/Ran_Wang_CV_Public.pdf){.btn .btn-primary}
:::
:::
